g.text: kong-mcp-oauth2 · Unified MCP OAuth Front Door
A Kong go-pdk plugin (mcp-oauth2) that puts ONE OAuth front door before every MCP server — verified locally with RS256 + JWKS, then forwarded upstream.
Kong ADVERTISES where the flow lives (steps ② ③) and VERIFIES the token that comes back (step ⑤) — it never runs OAuth. The MCP client drives Auth Code + PKCE against AuthGate itself.
RSA public keys → Kong verifies every token offline
⑥ 200 OK ← relayed back to the client
• Gateway never holds a signing secret
— it only ever sees the public key.
the JWKS in the background, no Kong edit.
• Alg-confusion blocked: HS* is refused, so
One plugin config protects one MCP resource — attach it to gitea, sentry, … : same OAuth front door, different resource_path + required_scopes.
